inputs.description: User natural-language task description (passed verbatim from external taskDescription input). Renamed from "description" to disambiguate from analyzing.outputs.pipelineDescription — see dogfood Finding 7 (2026-04-25).
inputs.name: taskText

inputs.description: User free-text correction supplied at the awaitingConfirm gate via answer_gate({answer:"reject", comment:<this>}). Empty string on the first pass before the gate has fired; populated on reject-rerun. The analyzing agent must read this and, when non-empty, adjust its design accordingly.
inputs.name: rejectionFeedback

outputs.description: Investigation sub-type (4 literal strings); drives skeleton variant + Layer 3 reportJudge thresholds.
outputs.name: investigationType

outputs.description: Audience model: role, what they know/don't know/care about.
outputs.name: audience
outputs.type: { role: string; knowsAbout: string[]; doesNotKnow: string[]; caresAbout: string[] }

outputs.description: Registrable domain of the primary subject (e.g. '0g.ai'); empty when no single subject.
outputs.name: subjectDomain

outputs.description: Layer 1 prerequisite concepts with tier + dependencies.
outputs.name: concepts
outputs.type: Array<{ name: string; tier: "core" | "support" | "optional"; deps: string[] }>

outputs.description: Human-readable name for the new investigation pipeline.
outputs.name: pipelineName

outputs.description: 1-2 sentence description of what the pipeline does.
outputs.name: pipelineDescription

outputs.description: External MCP server entries (verbatim from catalog) attached to evidenceGather; empty array for most topics.
outputs.name: recommendedMcps
outputs.type: Array<{ entryId: string; name: string; command: string; args: string[]; env?: Record<string, string>; envKeys: string[]; reason: string }>

outputs.description: Markdown summary shown at awaitingConfirm gate for user review.
outputs.name: summary

outputs.description: Markdown design narrative explaining the investigation choices.
outputs.name: stageDesign

subAgents.description: Generates a single system prompt for one kernel-next agent stage.
subAgents.name: prompt-writer
subAgents.prompt:
You write system prompts for workflow-control kernel-next agent stages. Given a stage specification (name, inputs ports, outputs ports, purpose, budget, whether it invokes run_pipeline), produce a high-quality system prompt in markdown.

Required structure: role definition (1-2 sentences), Available Inputs section listing each input port with its type and meaning, step-by-step Workflow with action verbs, explicit write_port calls for each output port using literal syntax, and Error Handling section with concrete fallbacks.

Critical rules:
- 30-80 lines per prompt. Under 30 is too vague, over 80 dilutes attention.
- Do NOT include a JSON output schema section — writes go through write_port MCP tool calls, which the prompt must show literal examples of.
- If the stage invokes a sub-pipeline, include explicit run_pipeline instructions with the exact sub-pipeline name and input shape.
- Use specific roles ("security auditor scanning OWASP vulnerabilities") not generic ones ("AI assistant").
- Do not fabricate output ports that are not declared in the stage spec.
- TEMPORAL ANCHOR (F20, 2026-04-26): if the stage performs research, fact-checking, or claim verification, include in Workflow an explicit instruction: "Treat the current system date as the report date. Any source claim dated AFTER the report date is a projection/forecast, NOT an established fact — flag it explicitly with a [Projection — dated YYYY-MM-DD] marker. Do NOT fold future-dated events into the deliverable as established facts and do NOT raise confidence on the basis of post-dated sources." This rule prevents hallucinated 'recent events' from leaking into research deliverables and corrupting the confidence scoring.

outputs.description: Assembled 17-stage PipelineIR (deterministic; passes submit_pipeline by construction).
outputs.name: ir

outputs.description: Sub-pipeline IRs (always empty for investigation; the 17-stage skeleton is single-pipeline).
outputs.name: subIrs

inputs.description: PipelineIR JSON object emitted by genSkeleton (raw, possibly with mechanical errors that the LLM made — see continuation 9.5 handoff for the 5 known classes).
inputs.name: ir

inputs.description: Sub-pipeline IRs from genSkeleton, repaired independently.
inputs.name: subIrs

outputs.description: PipelineIR after deterministic post-processing: dedup wires, fuzzy-match port-name typos, repair fanout-output shape, backfill externalInputs[]. genPrompts reads this so prompts are written against the corrected IR.
outputs.name: ir

outputs.description: Sub-pipeline IRs after the same repairs.
outputs.name: subIrs

outputs.description: Human-readable list of every repair the script applied. Empty when the LLM IR was already clean. Surfaced to the dashboard so users see what was auto-corrected vs what came from the LLM verbatim.
outputs.name: repairs

validateAndRepairIR.ir.description: Repaired PipelineIR. Output of validate_and_repair_ir builtin: structurally valid (5 mechanical-error classes auto-fixed), consumed by genPrompts and persisting downstream.

validateAndRepairIR.repairs.description: Human-readable list of repairs applied to the LLM-emitted IR. Surfaced in dashboard so users can audit what was auto-corrected vs LLM-authored.

validateAndRepairIR.subIrs.description: Repaired sub-pipeline IRs (each independently passed through validate_and_repair_ir).
